article_title: Is Andy Burnham promising a new dawn for North Sea oil and gas?

tasks.prompt: What question does the article examine about Andy Burnham’s energy policy?
options: Whether he plans to close every British power station immediately
Whether his approach could bring renewed support for North Sea oil and gas while reshaping the energy transition
Whether Scotland will leave the United Kingdom over fishing rights
Whether household electricity will become free next year
tasks.correct_answer: Whether his approach could bring renewed support for North Sea oil and gas while reshaping the energy transition
tasks.explanation_hebrew: הכתבה בוחנת האם עמדתו של אנדי ברנהאם מסמנת תמיכה מחודשת בנפט ובגז בים הצפוני לצד שינוי בדגש של מדיניות המעבר האנרגטי.

tasks.prompt: Which policy tension is central to the article?
options: Reducing university fees while increasing defence spending
Choosing between road building and rail fares
Supporting jobs and domestic energy production while meeting climate and transition goals
Balancing tourism with agricultural exports
tasks.correct_answer: Supporting jobs and domestic energy production while meeting climate and transition goals
tasks.explanation_hebrew: המתח המרכזי הוא בין תמיכה בתעסוקה ובהפקת אנרגיה מקומית לבין עמידה ביעדי אקלים ומעבר לאנרגיה נקייה.

tasks.prompt: What distinction does the article make about North Sea activity?
options: Exploration and production are exactly the same legal process
Oil and gas can only be produced on land
All existing licences have already expired
Honouring existing licences is different from issuing unrestricted new licences
tasks.correct_answer: Honouring existing licences is different from issuing unrestricted new licences
tasks.explanation_hebrew: הכתבה מבחינה בין כיבוד רישיונות קיימים לבין שינוי מדיניות שיאפשר הנפקה רחבה של רישיונות חדשים.

tasks.prompt: Why does existing North Sea infrastructure matter to the debate?
options: Pipelines, platforms and supply chains may affect the cost and feasibility of future projects
It prevents any offshore wind development
It determines the price of imported food
It belongs entirely to foreign governments
tasks.correct_answer: Pipelines, platforms and supply chains may affect the cost and feasibility of future projects
tasks.explanation_hebrew: תשתיות קיימות כמו צינורות, אסדות ושרשראות אספקה משפיעות על הכדאיות והעלות של פרויקטים עתידיים.

tasks.prompt: In the article, what does a “pragmatic” approach mean?
options: A policy based only on symbolism
A refusal to consider economic evidence
A promise that cannot be changed under any circumstances
A practical approach focused on workable outcomes
tasks.correct_answer: A practical approach focused on workable outcomes
tasks.explanation_hebrew: Pragmatic פירושו גישה מעשית המתמקדת במה שניתן לבצע ובתוצאות אפשריות.

tasks.prompt: Which sentence best captures the article’s cautious conclusion?
options: The energy transition has been abandoned by all major parties
Burnham’s language suggests a change of emphasis, but it may be premature to call it a complete reversal of policy
A final decision has already reopened every part of the North Sea
North Sea production can expand without economic or environmental trade-offs
tasks.correct_answer: Burnham’s language suggests a change of emphasis, but it may be premature to call it a complete reversal of policy
tasks.explanation_hebrew: המסקנה זהירה: יש שינוי בדגש ובשפה, אך מוקדם לקבוע שמדובר בהיפוך מלא של המדיניות.

tasks.prompt: Give two economic arguments used by supporters of continued North Sea production.
tasks.rubric_hebrew: יש לציין שני טיעונים כגון שמירת משרות, שימוש בתשתיות קיימות, הכנסות ממסים, ביטחון אנרגטי או תמיכה בתעשייה המקומית.
expected_points: Protect jobs and specialist supply chains

tasks.prompt: Why might environmental groups view a new emphasis on drilling with concern?
tasks.rubric_hebrew: יש להסביר שקידוחים חדשים עלולים להאריך תלות בדלקים מאובנים, להקשות על עמידה ביעדי אקלים ולהסיט השקעות מאנרגיה נקייה.
expected_points: New projects could prolong fossil-fuel dependence
Additional production may conflict with climate targets
Capital and political attention could shift away from clean energy
Long-lived infrastructure can lock in emissions

tasks.prompt: Write a 3-4 sentence summary of Burnham’s position and the debate it has created.
tasks.rubric_hebrew: יש להציג את הדגש המעשי של ברנהאם, את הוויכוח על רישיונות וקידוחים, את הטיעונים הכלכליים ואת ההסתייגויות הסביבתיות.
expected_points: Burnham presents a more pragmatic or industry-friendly emphasis
The debate concerns existing and possible future North Sea activity
Supporters stress jobs, infrastructure and energy security
Critics stress climate commitments and transition risks
The final policy direction remains uncertain or under consultation

tasks.prompt: Write a balanced policy paragraph on whether limited North Sea production can coexist with a clean-energy transition.
tasks.rubric_hebrew: יש להציג טיעון בעד וטיעון נגד, להבחין בין פתרון קצר טווח למדיניות ארוכת טווח, ולסיים בעמדה מנומקת.
expected_points: Argument for production: jobs, security or use of existing assets
Argument against: emissions, lock-in or delayed clean investment
A distinction between short-term transition needs and long-term goals
